title: Slow-cooker lamb shanks in red wine
subtitle: One shank per person, braised until falling off the bone
excerpt: Lamb shanks browned and braised in red wine and beef stock with garlic, rosemary, and tomato puree for eight hours until the meat slides from the bone. The braising liquid reduces to a glossy, deeply flavoured sauce. One shank per person.
sourceNotes: Braised lamb shanks became a British restaurant staple in the 1990s and moved into home cooking shortly after. The slow cooker version follows the same logic as a Dutch-oven braise: browning for depth, red wine for body, and enough time for the collagen in the shank to dissolve into the sauce. The result is a dish that looks impressive and requires almost no skill.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze shanks with their sauce. Reheat covered at 160°C for 40 minutes or gently on the hob.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. Refrigerate in the sauce and skim the solidified fat before reheating.

content.text: Brown the shanks before they go in. Lamb shank is dense and the browning on the outside adds colour to the finished sauce that the slow cooker cannot provide on its own. Three minutes per side in a hot pan is enough — you are adding colour, not cooking them through.

Season the {{lamb-shanks}} all over with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a wide pan over high heat and brown the shanks on all sides — 3 minutes per side. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{onion}}, {{carrot}}, {{garlic}}, {{rosemary}}, and {{bay-leaves}} around the shanks. Stir the {{tomato-puree}} into the {{red-wine}} and pour over, then add the {{stock-beef}}. The liquid should come about halfway up the shanks. Cook on low for 8 hours, until the meat pulls away from the bone easily. Transfer the shanks carefully. Strain the cooking juices into a saucepan and reduce over high heat for 10 minutes to a glossy sauce.

items.symptom: Sauce thin and wine-tasting after cooking
items.cause: Not reduced after the slow cooker stage
items.fix: Pour the braising liquid into a wide saucepan and boil hard for 10 minutes. The alcohol cooks off and the sauce concentrates to a glossy consistency

items.cause: Slow cooker running below temperature, or very large shanks
items.fix: Continue cooking for another 1 to 2 hours. Lamb shank collagen needs time to break down — rushing it produces a tough result

items.symptom: Meat falling off the bone before serving
items.fix: Lift the shanks carefully with a slotted spoon and place them on a warm tray. They will hold together while you reduce the sauce

content.text: Lamb shanks are a restaurant dish at home prices. The slow cooker removes all technical skill from the equation — the only moment that requires attention is the browning, which takes ten minutes. Serve on mashed potato with the reduced sauce poured over and braised vegetables alongside.
